Ayahs

قَدْ أَفْلَحَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (۱)
Certainly, the faithful have attained salvation (1)
ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ فِى صَلَاتِهِمْ خَـٰشِعُونَ (۲)
—those who are humble in their prayers, (2)

Key Points

In the Quran, the word falah (salvation/success) is accompanied by the word la'alla (perhaps/so that) in eleven instances: «لعلّكم تفلحون» (so that you may succeed). However, in three instances, it is mentioned without this word and definitively: one is in this very Surah, another is in Surah Ash-Shams: «قد أَفلح مَن زكّاها» (He has certainly succeeded who purifies it), and the third is in Surah Al-A'la: «قد اَفلح مَن تَزكّى» (He has certainly succeeded who purifies himself). By comparing these three instances, it becomes clear that faith (Iman) and purification (Tazkiyah) possess a single essence.

The highest and ultimate goal of all Islamic programs is falah (success) and salvation. Many verses of the Quran speak of piety (Taqwa), and the goal of religious rulings is to achieve Taqwa: «لعلّكم تتّقون» [51] (so that you may become righteous). And the ultimate goal of Taqwa is also falah: «واتّقوا اللّه لعلّكم تفلحون» [52] (and fear Allah so that you may succeed).

It is interesting that the most important and ultimate goal of the religion of Islam is mentioned in its very first slogan: «قولوا لا اله الاّ اللّه تفلحوا» (Say, 'There is no god but Allah,' and you will succeed). Believe in the Oneness of Allah and say «لا اله الاّ اللّه» so that you may achieve salvation. Every day and night, in the Adhan (call to prayer) and Iqamah (establishment of prayer) for the daily prayers, we utter the phrase «حَىّ على الفَلاح» (Hasten to success) twenty times, loudly proclaiming the ultimate goal so that we do not lose our way.

Islam says: «قد أفلح المؤمنون» (The believers have certainly succeeded), «قد أفلَح مَن تَزكّى » [53] (He has certainly succeeded who purifies himself)—those who have faith and self-purification are successful. But disbelief (Kufr) says: «قد أفلح الیوم مَنِ استعلى» [54] (He has certainly succeeded today who prevails). Pharaoh used to say: Today, whoever has power and superiority is successful.

The word falah means to sprout or grow. Perhaps the reason a farmer is called a fallah is that he provides the means for the seed to sprout. When a seed is placed in the soil, it saves itself and reaches the open space through three actions: First, it anchors its roots deep into the earth. Second, it absorbs the nutrients from the earth. Third, it pushes away the obstructing soil. Yes, for man to free himself, reach the open space of Monotheism (Tawhid), and be saved from the darkness of materialism, desires, and tyrants (Taghuts), he must perform those same three actions like the seed: First, he must strengthen the roots of his beliefs through reasoning. Second, he must absorb from the God-given resources whatever is necessary for his spiritual perfection and growth. Third, he must push aside all enemies and obstructors and repel every deity besides Allah with the phrase «لا اله» (There is no god) until he reaches the open space of Monotheism.

A person was playing with his beard while praying. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: If he had humility (Khushu') in his heart, he would not pray in this manner in action. [55]

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of Khushu' in prayer is lowering the gaze. [56]

Who are the successful? 1. The worshippers. «واعبدوا ربّكم وافعلوا الخیر لعلّكم تفلحون» [57] (Worship your Lord and do good so that you may succeed). 2. Those whose deeds are valuable and heavy. «فَمَن ثَقُلت مَوازینه فاولئك هم المفلِحون» [58] 3. Those who avoid stinginess. «و مَن یُوقَ شُحَّ نفسه فاولئك هم المفلِحون» [59] 4. The Party of Allah (Hizbullah). «اَلا اِنّ حزب اللّه هم المفلِحون» [60] The Party of Allah are the successful. 5. The people of abundant remembrance (Dhikr). «واذكروا اللّه كثیرا لعلّكم تفلحون» [61] Remember Allah often so that you may succeed. 6. The pious. «فاتّقوا اللّه یا اولى الالباب لعلّكم تُفلِحون» [62] (So fear Allah, O people of understanding, that you may succeed). O wise ones! Be pious so that you may succeed. 7. The strivers (Mujahidin). «و جاهدوا فى سَبیله لعلّكم تُفلحون» [63] Strive in the way of Allah so that you may succeed. 8. The repentant. «تُوبوا الى اللّه جمیعاً ایّها المؤمنون لعلّكم تُفلحون» [64] O people of faith! Repent all together so that you may succeed.

51) Baqarah, 183. 52) Aal-i Imran, 130. 53) A'la, 14. 54) Taha, 64. 55) Tafsir Kanz ad-Daqa'iq. 56) Bihar, Vol. 81, p. 264. 57) Hajj, 77. 58) A'raf, 8. 59) Hashr, 9. 60) Mujadilah, 22. 61) Anfal, 45. 62) Ma'idah, 100. 63) Ma'idah, 35. 64) Nur, 31.

Messages

  1. The success of the believers is certain. «قد أفلح»
  2. Faith has conditions and signs. «المؤمنون الّذین...»
  3. Prayer is at the forefront of Islamic programs. «فى صلاتهم»
  4. In prayer, the state and quality are important. «خاشعون»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Mu'minun

This Surah, which has 118 verses, was revealed in Mecca. Given the description of the characteristics of the believers and the effects and signs of faith in the initial verses of this Surah, the name "Al-Mu'minun" (The Believers) was chosen for it. Of course, the stories of Prophets such as Noah, Hud, Moses, and Jesus عليهم السلام and the responses of various nations to their calls are mentioned later on.

Regarding the virtue of reciting this Surah, Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: Continuously reciting this Surah, especially on Fridays, leads to happiness in this world and the Hereafter and companionship with the Divine Prophets in the highest Paradise (Firdaws). (50)

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ عَنِ ٱللَّغْوِ مُعْرِضُونَ (۳)
avoid vain talk, (3)
وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِلزَّكَوٰةِ فَـٰعِلُونَ (٤)
carry out their [duty of] zakat, (4)

Key Points

|

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِفُرُوجِهِمْ حَـٰفِظُونَ (٥)
guard their private parts, (5)
إِلَّا عَلَىٰٓ أَزْوَٰجِهِمْ أَوْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ غَيْرُ مَلُومِينَ (٦)
(except from their spouses or their slave women, for then they are not blameworthy; (6)

Key Points

Laghw (vain talk/action) refers to useless work and speech. Avoiding and refraining from laghw is not exclusive to Muslims. The Quran also says about the righteous People of the Book: «و اذا سَمعوا اللغو اعرضوا عنه...» [65] The believing People of the Book shun laghw. Zakat (alms) linguistically means growth, development, and purity. Technically (in religious terminology), it is a type of religious tax and one of the pillars and obligations of the religion. In Islamic society, safeguarding prayer (salat) and chastity (namus) is very important. In these very few verses, chastity is mentioned using the term hāfiẓūn (those who guard), and prayer is mentioned using the phrase «على صلوتهم یحافظون» (they guard their prayers). Avoiding laghw in this world results in being protected from laghw in the Hereafter. «عن اللغو معرضون» (They turn away from laghw), «لا تسمع فیها لاغیه» [66] (They shall not hear therein any vain talk). Turning away from laghw should not be accompanied by slander or harshness, because in another verse He says: «و اذا مرّوا باللغو مرّوا كراما» [67] (And when they pass by vain talk, they pass by with dignity). In narrations, examples of laghw are mentioned, including: speech in which there is no remembrance of Allah, or which involves singing (ghinā) and amusements (lahwiyyāt). [68] We read in narrations: Do not marry anyone except a chaste woman, and do not share a bed with someone whom you do not trust with your wealth. [69]

Importance of Zakat

Zakat is one of the five things upon which the structure of Islam is founded. The word Zakat appears 32 times in the Quran, and the word Barak (blessing) appears 32 times, as if Zakat is equivalent to blessing. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله expelled individuals who did not pay Zakat from the mosque. Imam Mahdi عليه السلام will fight those who abandon Zakat during His great revolution. Whoever does not pay Zakat will be told at the time of his death: Die as a Jew or a Christian. The one who abandons Zakat asks Allah at the moment of death to return him to the world so that he may perform good deeds, but his respite has ended. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If people paid Zakat, no poor person would remain on earth.

65) Qasas, 52 to 55. 66) Ghashiyah, 11. 67) Furqan, 72. 68) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 69) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 453.

Messages

1- The sign of faith is avoiding laghw. «والّذینهم عن اللغو معرضون» (A believer must have a correct purpose in all his actions and speech.)

2- Avoiding laghw is so important that it is mentioned between prayer (salat) and Zakat. «خاشعون - معرضون - للزّكوة فاعلون»

3- The importance of Zakat is like the importance of prayer. (In 28 verses, prayer and Zakat are mentioned side by side.) «فى صلوتهم خاشعون، للزّكوة فاعلون»

4- Belief alone is not enough; action is also necessary. «المؤمنون... فاعلون»

5- Modesty (hayā) is a condition of faith. «لفروجهم حافظون» (Sexual impurity is a sign of weak faith.)

6- Lust is rebellious and requires control and protection. «حافظون»

7- Restriction is necessary in satisfying the sexual instinct, but prohibition is contrary to nature (fitrah). «الاّ على ازواجهم»

8- Improper modesty is forbidden. Once Islam permits an action, all traditions, customs, and personal preferences that oppose the religion are condemned. «غیر ملومین»

Ayahs

فَمَنِ ٱبْتَغَىٰ وَرَآءَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْعَادُونَ (۷)
but whoever seeks [anything] beyond that—it is they who are transgressors), (7)
وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِأَمَـٰنَـٰتِهِمْ وَعَهْدِهِمْ رَٰعُونَ (۸)
and those who keep their trusts and covenants (8)

Key Points

|

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ عَلَىٰ صَلَوَٰتِهِمْ يُحَافِظُونَ (۹)
and are watchful of their prayers. (9)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْوَٰرِثُونَ (۱٠)
It is they who will be the inheritors, (10)

Key Points

|

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسَ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۱۱)
who shall inherit paradise and will remain in it [forever]. (11)

Key Points

The word Firdaws (فردوس) means garden, and it is the highest place in Paradise. (70) Since it is used instead of Jannah (Paradise), a feminine pronoun is used for it [in the original Arabic context].

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام recited Ayah 7 in response to a ruling regarding masturbation. (71)

The Importance of Trustworthiness (Amanah) in Narrations Do not look at people's prayers, fasting, or Hajj; look at their truthfulness and trustworthiness. (72)

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, in the final moments of His life, said to Hadrat Ali عليه السلام: «ادّ الامانة الى البّر و الفاجر فیما قلّ و جلّ حتّى الخیط و المخیط» Return the trust (Amanah) to its owner, whether he is righteous or sinful, whether it is valuable or insignificant, even if it is a piece of thread or a sewn cloth/garment. (73)

All material, spiritual, and political blessings are divine trusts (Amanah). (Imam Khomeini قدس سره said: The Islamic Republic is a divine trust.)

The Noble Quran has praised some of the People of the Book who are trustworthy: «و من اهل الكتاب من ان تأمنه بِقنطار یُؤدّه الیك» (74) If you entrust a great amount of wealth to some of the People of the Book, they will return it to you.

70) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 71) Wasā’il, Vol. 18, p. 575. 72) Mizan al-Hikmah and Safinat al-Bihar, entry "Amanah". 73) Bihar, Vol. 77, p. 273. 74) Āl ‘Imrān, 75.

Messages

1- Lawbreaking is transgression against the limits that Allah has set. «العادون»

2- Seeking out sin is also a sin. «ابتغى - عادون»

3- A believer is committed. «عهدهم راعون» Breaking a promise is a sign of weak faith.

4- Observing covenants and agreements is necessary, even if we cannot fulfill them completely in practice. «راعون» (observing/guarding) not «موفون» (fulfilling).

5- In describing the characteristics of the believers, prayer (Salat) was mentioned both at the beginning: «فى صلوتهم خاشعون» and at the end: «على صلوتهم یحافظون». This repetition and emphasis demonstrate the importance of prayer.

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِن سُلَـٰلَةٍ مِّن طِينٍ (۱۲)
Certainly We created man from an extract of clay. (12)
ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ نُطْفَةً فِى قَرَارٍ مَّكِينٍ (۱۳)
Then We made him a drop of [seminal] fluid [lodged] in a secure abode. (13)

Key Points

|

Ayah

ثُمَّ خَلَقْنَا ٱلنُّطْفَةَ عَلَقَةً فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْعَلَقَةَ مُضْغَةً فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْمُضْغَةَ عِظَـٰمًا فَكَسَوْنَا ٱلْعِظَـٰمَ لَحْمًا ثُمَّ أَنشَأْنَـٰهُ خَلْقًا ءَاخَرَ ۚ فَتَبَارَكَ ٱللَّهُ أَحْسَنُ ٱلْخَـٰلِقِينَ (۱٤)
Then We created the drop of fluid as a clinging mass. Then We created the clinging mass as a fleshy tissue. Then We created the fleshy tissue as bones. Then We clothed the bones with flesh. Then We produced him as [yet] another creature. So blessed is Allah, the best of creators! (14)

Key Points

The word sulālah means essence (or extract), the word ‘alaqah means thick, clotted blood, and the word muḍghah means a piece of flesh without bone. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of khalqan ākhar (another creation) is the blowing of the soul into the body. [75] The meaning of aḥsan al-khāliqīn (the Best of Creators), according to Imam Reza عليه السلام, is that Allah has also given the power of creation to others; for example, Prophet Jesus creates by the permission of Allah. [76] «أخلق لكم من الطین كهیئة الطّیر» [77] The proofs that Allah is the Best of Creators are numerous, including: A: His creativity is perpetual. B: He is not limited to specific objects. C: The variety in it is unlimited. D: He creates the most important things from the simplest materials. E: His primary materials originate from Him. F: He does not imitate in creation. G: Regret and doubt have no place in Him. H: He creates based on mercy, and all His creations are purposeful. I: His creation is harmonious with the cosmic order.

75) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 76) Bihar, Vol. 4, p. 147. 77) Al Imran, 49.

Messages

1- Remembering man's past and weaknesses, and paying attention to Allah's power and grace, is a foundation for strengthening faith and increasing worship. laqad khalaqnā (We have certainly created).

2- The creation of man has taken place in several stages. ṭīn (clay) - nuṭfah (sperm-drop) - ‘alaqah (clot)...

3- Despite the mother having various movements, her womb is a secure resting place for the child. qarārin makīn (a secure resting place).

4- All stages of creation are under the supervision of Allah. khalaqnā (We created) - kasawnā (We clothed) - ansha’nā (We brought forth).

5- The value of man lies in his soul. khalqan ākhar (another creation).

6- Self-knowledge is the prelude to the knowledge of Allah. nuṭfah, ‘alaqah, muḍghah (sperm-drop, clot, piece of flesh) - fatabāraka Allāhu aḥsanul-khāliqīn (So blessed is Allah, the Best of Creators).

Ayahs

ثُمَّ إِنَّكُم بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ لَمَيِّتُونَ (۱٥)
Then indeed you die after that. (15)
ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ تُبْعَثُونَ (۱٦)
Then you will indeed be raised up on the Day of Resurrection. (16)

Key Points

Allah has considered dying certain and inevitable in these verses. Here, mentioning a saying from Amir al-Mu'minin عليه السلام is appropriate: «فلو أنّ احداً یجد الى البقاء سُلّماً او لدفع الموت سبیلاً لكان ذلك سلیمان بن داوود علیه السلام الّذى سُخّر له ملك الجنّ و الانس، مع النبوّة و عظیم الزلفة ... رمته قسى الفناء بنبال الموت». [۷۸] He said: If anyone could devise a means for eternal permanence in the world, or find a way to avert death, it would have been Sulayman son of Dawud عليه السلام, who ruled over the jinn and mankind, besides being a Prophet and possessing a great station and rank... (Nevertheless) the bows of annihilation struck him down with the arrows of death (and he departed from the world of existence). The previous verse described the evolutionary path of human creation, and Allah congratulated Himself (on this creation). These two verses also describe the final stages of evolution after death.

78) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 182.

Messages

  1. Death is not annihilation; it is a stage of evolution. «ثمّ انّكم بعد ذلك لمیّتون»

  2. Death is the certain destiny of man. «ثمّ انّكم ... لمیّتون» (The particle inna, the letter lām, and the nominal sentence structure all indicate emphasis.)

  3. The creation of man is purposeful. «ثمّ انّكم یوم القیامة تبعثون»

  4. There is a long interval between death and resurrection. (Thumma [ثمّ] indicates a long interval.)

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا فَوْقَكُمْ سَبْعَ طَرَآئِقَ وَمَا كُنَّا عَنِ ٱلْخَلْقِ غَـٰفِلِينَ (۱۷)
Certainly We created above you seven levels and We have not been oblivious of the creation. (17)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning of the word ṭarā'iq (طرائق) is the path of the angels' passage, or the path and orbit of the movement of the celestial bodies. It is also possible that the intent is the seven heavens which have paths connecting them. The previous verses discussed the creation of man, and this verse discusses the creation of the heavens.

Messages

1- Allah is both the Creator and the Observer. (The universe is in the presence of Allah.) «وما كنّا عن الخلق غافلین»

2- The creation and governance of the universe requires constant management. «ماكنّاعن الخلق غافلین»

Ayah

وَأَنزَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۢ بِقَدَرٍ فَأَسْكَنَّـٰهُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَإِنَّا عَلَىٰ ذَهَابٍۭ بِهِۦ لَقَـٰدِرُونَ (۱۸)
We sent down water from the sky in a measured manner, and We lodged it within the ground, and We are indeed able to take it away. (18)

Messages

1- The power of Allah is equal in granting blessings and taking them away. «انزلنا - ذهاب»

2- Raindrops are measured and calculated. «بقدر» (Rain is not a natural phenomenon that is aimless or uncalculated).

3- The storage of water is among the Divine favors and a sign that He is not neglectful of creation. «و ماكنّا عن الخَلقِ غافلین و انزلنا... فاسكناه»

4- Allah rules over phenomena. «و اِنّا على ذهاب به»

5- Let us not think that blessings are permanent. «على ذهاب به لَقادرون»

Ayahs

فَأَنشَأْنَا لَكُم بِهِۦ جَنَّـٰتٍ مِّن نَّخِيلٍ وَأَعْنَـٰبٍ لَّكُمْ فِيهَا فَوَٰكِهُ كَثِيرَةٌ وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ (۱۹)
Then with it We produced for you gardens of date palms and vines. There are abundant fruits in them for you, and you eat from them, (19)
وَشَجَرَةً تَخْرُجُ مِن طُورِ سَيْنَآءَ تَنۢبُتُ بِٱلدُّهْنِ وَصِبْغٍ لِّلْـَٔاكِلِينَ (۲٠)
and a tree that grows on Mount Sinai, which produces oil and a seasoning for those who eat. (20)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Water is the source of life for plants and vegetation. «فاَنشأنا لكم به»

2- Trees and plants have been created for the benefit of human beings. (The word «لكم» (for you) has been used twice in Ayah 19).

3- Among the fruits, some, like dates and grapes, hold a special significance. «نَخیل واَعناب»

4- Allah, the Wise, has provided for the material needs of mankind. «نَخیل واَعناب»

5- Allah's production exceeds human consumption. «فَواكِهُ كثیرةٌ و منها تأكلون»

6- Whatever Allah has created is beneficial and blessed. «فَواكهُ كثیرة - تَأكلون - صِبغٍ» (But where there is harm and ruin, it is the work of man, just as we read in Ayah 67 of Surah An-Nahl: «تتّخذون منه سكراً» (You make intoxicants from it)).

7- Every region is suitable for a specific tree and plant. «تخرج من طورسیناء»

8- Some lands are blessed both spiritually and materially. (Mount Sinai is the place where revelation descended and where olive trees grow). «من طور سیناء»

9- Olive oil is one of the divine blessings. «تَنبُتُ بالدُّهن»

Ayahs

وَإِنَّ لَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ لَعِبْرَةً ۖ نُّسْقِيكُم مِّمَّا فِى بُطُونِهَا وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَنَـٰفِعُ كَثِيرَةٌ وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ (۲۱)
There is indeed a moral for you in the cattle: We give you to drink of that which is in their bellies, and you have many uses in them, and you eat some of them, (21)
وَعَلَيْهَا وَعَلَى ٱلْفُلْكِ تُحْمَلُونَ (۲۲)
and you are carried on them and on ships. (22)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Livestock (quadrupeds) were created for mankind. (The word "لكم" [for you] appears twice in the Ayah.)

2- We should not view even animals simply. It is "لَعِبرة" (a lesson). Extracting iron from the earth requires hundreds of experts, specialists, and tools, but Allah draws milk out from the heart of grass (plants), and with this same power, He will bring mankind forth from the earth on the Day of Resurrection. "لَعِبرَة"

3- Milk is one of the blessings to which the Quran draws attention. "نُسقیكم" (We give you to drink).

4- Fruits and vegetable oils are mentioned before animal foods. First, He named "نَخیل" (date-palms), "اَعناب" (grapes), "زَیتون" (olives), and then He said: "نُسقیكم" (We give you to drink), "تأكلون" (you eat).

5- Milk, meat, and riding are only a part of the benefits derived from animals. "لكم فیها منافعُ كثیرة" (In them are many benefits for you). The establishment of dairy factories, leather production, canning, spinning, weaving, and the creation of employment and productivity for millions of people are the secondary benefits of animals.

6- We should not view the role of ships in transportation simply. (Hundreds of formulas work together to make this possibility available.) "على الفلك تحملون" (and upon the ships you are carried).

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ فَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ ۖ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۲۳)
Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. Will you not then be wary [of Him]?’ (23)

Key Points

In the previous verses, Allah spoke about the creation of man and what pertains to securing his material interests, and in these verses, He addresses the provision of his intellectual and ideological sustenance.

Messages

1- In propagating the religion, address people with affection. «یا قوم»

2- Invitation to Monotheism (Tawhīd) is the first duty of the prophets. (In propagation, pay attention to the fundamental issues.) «اعبدوا اللّه»

3- The spirit of worship exists within the inner nature of man; the prophets clarify the object of worship. «اعبدوا اللّه - ما لكم من اله غیره»

4- Turning towards other than Allah is recklessness (or lack of piety). «أفلا تتّقون»

Ayah

فَقَالَ ٱلْمَلَؤُا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ مَا هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُرِيدُ أَن يَتَفَضَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَأَنزَلَ مَلَـٰٓئِكَةً مَّا سَمِعْنَا بِهَـٰذَا فِىٓ ءَابَآئِنَا ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۲٤)
But the elite of the faithless from among his people said, ‘This is just a human being like you, who seeks to dominate you. Had Allah wished, He would have sent down angels. We never heard of such a thing among our forefathers. (24)

Messages

1- The elite were in the front line of opposition to the prophets. «مَلَأ»

2- Self-love is a deep instinct in humans. (Man is concerned that someone might gain superiority over him.) «یتفضّل علیكم»

3- The accusation of seeking superiority is a tool used in the propaganda of the disbelievers. «یرید اَن یَتفضّل علیكم»

4- The arrogant person prescribes duties even for Allah. «لو شاءاللّه لانزل ملائكةً»

5- Disbelievers deem perfection to be a flaw. «ماهذا الاّ بشر... لانزل ملائكةً» (The fact that the prophets were human is a perfection necessary for guiding humanity, but the disbelievers said: He should not be human.)

6- In the face of sound logic, do not rely on history, the imitation of ancestors, or your national culture, and do not be a defender of the status quo. «ما سَمِعنا بهذا»

Ayahs

إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا رَجُلٌۢ بِهِۦ جِنَّةٌ فَتَرَبَّصُوا۟ بِهِۦ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ (۲٥)
He is just a man possessed by madness. So bear with him for a while.’ (25)
قَالَ رَبِّ ٱنصُرْنِى بِمَا كَذَّبُونِ (۲٦)
He said, ‘My Lord! Help me, for they impugn me.’ (26)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Hearing the history of past generations is a source of solace for future generations. (O Prophet! They also attributed madness to the previous prophets.) «رجل به جِنّة»

2- Some who cannot advance belittle the status of great figures. «رجل به جِنّة»

3- Arrogance and obstinacy cause the wisest of people to be accused of madness. «رجل به جِنّة»

4- The leaders of disbelief consider the invitation of the prophets to be a psychological incident and say: wait until his excitements and claims cease. «فتربّصوا»

5- Reliance (Tawakkul) and supplication (Du'a) are the greatest leverage against accusations and humiliations. «قال ربّ»

6- The request for help from Allah is for the victory of faith over disbelief, not the victory of an individual over another individual. «انصرنى بما كذّبون» He did not say: «انصرنى علیهم»

Ayah

فَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْهِ أَنِ ٱصْنَعِ ٱلْفُلْكَ بِأَعْيُنِنَا وَوَحْيِنَا فَإِذَا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا وَفَارَ ٱلتَّنُّورُ ۙ فَٱسْلُكْ فِيهَا مِن كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ ٱثْنَيْنِ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا مَن سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقَوْلُ مِنْهُمْ ۖ وَلَا تُخَـٰطِبْنِى فِى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ ۖ إِنَّهُم مُّغْرَقُونَ (۲۷)
So We revealed to him: ‘Build the ark before Our eyes and by Our revelation. When Our edict comes and the oven gushes [a stream of water], bring into it a pair of every kind [of animal], and your family, except those of them against whom the decree has gone beforehand, and do not plead with Me for those who are wrongdoers: they shall indeed be drowned.’ (27)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Prophet Noah عليه السلام invited the people to Monotheism secretly and openly for 950 years, until Allah told him with the phrase: «لن یؤمن مِن قومك الاّ مَن قد آمن» [79] (None of your people will believe except those who have already believed), then Noah said: Their descendants are also not worthy of guidance. «ولایلدوا الاّ فاجراًكفّارا» [80] Then Allah commanded the building of the Ark. [81]

79) Hud, 36. 80) Nuh, 27. 81) Bihar, Vol. 11, p. 331. (I wrote this Hadith of Imam Baqir عليه السلام in Medina beside His pure grave)

Messages

1- The prayers of the Prophets are answered. «ربّ انصرنى - فاوحینا»

2- Industry (or craftsmanship) is a Divine inspiration. «اوحینا - اَن اِصنع»

3- Requesting Divine help does not contradict human effort. «اِصنَع الفُلك» The Prophets worked alongside their prayers.

4- We should support those who are humiliated. (People consider you mad, but you are under Our supervision.) «باعیننا»

5- If we act according to revelation in all matters, we will succeed. «اِصنَع الفلك باعیننا و وحینا»

6- By the Will of Allah, water gushes forth from the oven of fire. «فارالتّنّور» (Perhaps the gushing of water from the oven was a sign of the time of the event.)

7- Do not let the species of animals become extinct. «من كلّ زوجین اثنین»

8- Even the wife and son of a Prophet, if they are unworthy, will be subject to Divine wrath. «الاّ من سبق علیه القول»

9- Intercession is not effective everywhere. «ولاتخاطبنى» The intercession of the Prophets is by Divine permission.

10- The polytheist is an oppressor (or wrongdoer). «فى الّذین ظلموا»

11- The consequence of oppression is destruction. «ظلموا - مغرقون»

12- Sometimes, water, which is the source of life, becomes the source of destruction. «مغرقون»

Ayah

فَإِذَا ٱسْتَوَيْتَ أَنتَ وَمَن مَّعَكَ عَلَى ٱلْفُلْكِ فَقُلِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى نَجَّىٰنَا مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۲۸)
‘When you, and those who are with you, are settled in the ark, say, ‘‘All praise belongs to Allah, who has delivered us from the wrongdoing lot.’’ (28)

Messages

1- Distance from oppressors and salvation from their dominance is one of the divine blessings. «الحمدللّه الّذى نجّانا»

2- Gratitude (Shukr) is necessary for every blessing. «الحمدللّه الّذى نجّانا»

3- The best formula (sighah) for gratitude is the blessed phrase «الحمدللّه».

4- Do not thank Allah because of the destruction and ruin of others, but rather thank Him for your own salvation. He said: «نجّانا» (He saved us), He did not say: «اهلكهم» (He destroyed them).

5- The ship is a means (wasilah), but salvation is the work of Allah. «نجّانا»

Ayahs

وَقُل رَّبِّ أَنزِلْنِى مُنزَلًا مُّبَارَكًا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلْمُنزِلِينَ (۲۹)
And say, ‘‘My Lord! Land me with a blessed landing, for You are the best of those who bring ashore.’’ ’ (29)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ وَإِن كُنَّا لَمُبْتَلِينَ (۳٠)
There are indeed signs in this; and indeed, We have been testing. (30)

Key Points

We read in a Hadith: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said to Ali عليه السلام: Whenever you enter a dwelling, recite Ayah (29) so that Allah may grant you the good of that place and keep its evil away from you. [82] Remembering Allah in every situation: In the call to Monotheism. «اعبدواللّه» [83] In building the Ark, through divine inspiration. «اِصنع الفلك باعیننا و وحینا» [84] In boarding, with praise to Allah. «فاذا استویت... فقل الحمدللّه» [85] In movement, with the Name of Allah. «بسم اللّه مَجراها و مُرساها» [86] In stopping, with supplication to Him. «ربّ اَنزلنى» [87]

82) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 83) Al-Mu’minūn, 23. 84) Al-Mu’minūn, 27. 85) Al-Mu’minūn, 28. 86) Hūd, 41. 87) Al-Mu’minūn, 29.

Messages

1- First, we should thank Allah for past blessings, and then ask Him for new blessings. «الحمدللّه الّذى - ربّ اَنزلنى»

2- In prayer and supplication, we should call upon Allah using the Name that is appropriate for our need. «اَنزلنى - خیرالمنزلین»

3- The historical accounts in the Quran are signs of guidance and a source of growth (not mere entertainment or memory). «لآیات»

4- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to test people in every situation, and the story of Noah is one example of divine testing. «مبتلین»

Ayahs

ثُمَّ أَنشَأْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْنًا ءَاخَرِينَ (۳۱)
Then after them We brought forth another generation, (31)
فَأَرْسَلْنَا فِيهِمْ رَسُولًا مِّنْهُمْ أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ ۖ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۳۲)
and We sent them an apostle from among themselves, saying, ‘Worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. Will you not then be wary [of Him]?’ (32)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Allah sent a prophet for every nation. «قَرناً آخرین فارسلنا»

2- The Prophet must be from among the people themselves. (So that they may have a practical and empathetic role model, and the proof against them is complete.) «منهم»

3- The worship of Allah is valuable when accompanied by the rejection of polytheism (Shirk). «أن اعبدوااللّه مالكم من اله غیره»

4- Inclination towards Monotheism (Tawhid) requires piety (Taqwa). «أن اعبدوا اللّه... افلا تتّقون»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِهِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِلِقَآءِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَأَتْرَفْنَـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا مَا هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يَأْكُلُ مِمَّا تَأْكُلُونَ مِنْهُ وَيَشْرَبُ مِمَّا تَشْرَبُونَ (۳۳)
Said the elite of his people, who were faithless and who denied the encounter of the Hereafter and whom We had given affluence in the life of the world: ‘This is just a human being like yourselves: he eats what you eat, and drinks what you drink. (33)

Messages

1- The invitation of the prophets is based on liberating people from the domination of the elite and the oppressors, and for this reason, the elite oppose the prophets more than others. «قال الملأ»

2- Since the opponents do not dare to deny the logic and principle of the prophets' reforms, they say: the prophets are not worthy of those reforms. «بشر مثلكم»

3- The life of the prophets is an ordinary life. «یأكل - یشرب»

4- The elite are heedless of spiritual and scientific matters. «یأكل - یشرب» (Amir al-Mu'minin عليه السلام says: Their envy caused them to utter these words. [88])

88) Bihar, Vol. 32, p. 97.

Ayahs

وَلَئِنْ أَطَعْتُم بَشَرًا مِّثْلَكُمْ إِنَّكُمْ إِذًا لَّخَـٰسِرُونَ (۳٤)
If you obey a human being like yourselves, you will indeed be losers. (34)
أَيَعِدُكُمْ أَنَّكُمْ إِذَا مِتُّمْ وَكُنتُمْ تُرَابًا وَعِظَـٰمًا أَنَّكُم مُّخْرَجُونَ (۳٥)
Does he promise you that when you have died and become bones and dust you will indeed be raised [from the dead]? (35)

Key Points

|

Ayah

۞ هَيْهَاتَ هَيْهَاتَ لِمَا تُوعَدُونَ (۳٦)
Far-fetched, far-fetched is what you are promised! (36)

Key Points

In the Quran, the word "هیهات" (Hayhāt) is used twice, specifically in this very Ayah, where the leaders of disbelief consider the occurrence of the Resurrection (Qiyāmah) to be very distant.

Slogans of the Disbelievers

  1. The Prophet is an ordinary human. «بشراً»
  2. He has no distinction over others. «مثلكم»
  3. Following the Prophets is a loss. «لخاسرون»
  4. The Resurrection and the second life are distant. «هیهات هیهات» (Because the Prophets repeatedly and always promised the Resurrection, «توعدون», the opponents also repeated the word "هیهات".)

What was mentioned are some of the stubborn or deceptive slogans of the disbelievers and those who fight against the truth. Through extensive propaganda, they deem following the Prophets a loss, but their goal is to make people obedient to themselves and, under the slogan of freedom, drag nations into slavery.

Ayahs

إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا ٱلدُّنْيَا نَمُوتُ وَنَحْيَا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَبْعُوثِينَ (۳۷)
There is nothing but the life of this world: we live and die, and we will not be resurrected. (37)
إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا رَجُلٌ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا وَمَا نَحْنُ لَهُۥ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ (۳۸)
He is just a man who has fabricated a lie against Allah, and we will not believe in him.’ (38)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Arrogant individuals do not conceive of any path other than their own. «اِن هى»

2- The short-sighted (focused only on immediate needs) will not be far-sighted (focused on the end result). «الاّ حیاتنا الدنیا» (Materialists are the short-minded people of society.)

3- The disbelievers accepted God (Allah) but did not accept the Resurrection (Ma'ad) or Prophethood (Nubuwwah). «افترى على اللّه»

4- The people of disbelief reject the divine saints (Awliya) and divine promises under the slogan of "preserving the sanctity and sacredness of Allah." «افترى على اللّه»

5- Sometimes society declines to such an extent that the true defenders of religion are introduced as liars, and the arrogant disbelievers are introduced as the defenders of the sanctity of God (Allah). «افترى على اللّه»

Ayahs

قَالَ رَبِّ ٱنصُرْنِى بِمَا كَذَّبُونِ (۳۹)
He said, ‘My Lord! Help me, for they impugn me.’ (39)
قَالَ عَمَّا قَلِيلٍ لَّيُصْبِحُنَّ نَـٰدِمِينَ (٤٠)
Said He, ‘In a little while they will become regretful.’ (40)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- The reliance of the prophets is Allah. «رَبِّ»

2- The word «رَبِّ» is the best word for supplication (Du'a). After the word «اللّهِ», the most frequent word in the Quran is the word «رَبِّ», and it is usually used in all supplications.

3- The disbelievers and the criminals will soon regret. (Consciences will awaken one day.) «نادِمِینَ»

Ayahs

فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ بِٱلْحَقِّ فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ غُثَآءً ۚ فَبُعْدًا لِّلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٤۱)
So the Cry seized them justly and We turned them into a scum. So away with the wrongdoing lot! (41)
ثُمَّ أَنشَأْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ قُرُونًا ءَاخَرِينَ (٤۲)
Then after them We brought forth other generations. (42)

Messages

1- The narration of the history and destruction of previous nations is a source of tranquility for the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله and a means of threat for the disbelievers of Mecca. «اخذتهم الصیحة ...»

2- The supplication of the prophets is answered. «ربّ - فاخذتهم»

3- Allah's wrath is based on justice. «الصیحةبالحقّ»

4- Opponents of the prophets suffer both spiritual torment, «نادمین», and physical torment, «غثاء», and they will also be afflicted in the Hereafter.

5- Allah's wrath is crushing. «غثاء»

6- Cursing the oppressors is permissible. «فبُعداً»

7- The tradition of Allah, after the extinction of one nation, is the creation of another nation. «ثمّ انشأنا»

(Historical transformations are under the sovereignty of Allah)

Ayah

مَا تَسْبِقُ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلَهَا وَمَا يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ (٤۳)
No nation can advance its time nor can it defer it. (43)

Messages

1- Every society and nation has a specific respite and a wise schedule. «ما تسبق ...»

2- Do not be deceived by the delay of Divine punishment. «ما تسبق ...»

3- Divine plans are not annulled by the clamor of individuals, and the Divine order governs historical transformations. «ما تسبق ...»

4- History has fixed traditions. «ما تسبق ...»

Ayah

ثُمَّ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلَنَا تَتْرَا ۖ كُلَّ مَا جَآءَ أُمَّةً رَّسُولُهَا كَذَّبُوهُ ۚ فَأَتْبَعْنَا بَعْضَهُم بَعْضًا وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ أَحَادِيثَ ۚ فَبُعْدًا لِّقَوْمٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ (٤٤)
Then We sent Our apostles successively. Whenever there came to a nation its apostle, they impugned him, so We made them follow one another [to extinction] and We turned them into folktales. So away with the faithless lot! (44)

Key Points

The word Ahadith (احادیث), the plural of Uhduthah (اُحدوثه), refers to astonishing stories that are narrated with great embellishment and listened to with interest.

Messages

1- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to send Prophets for the nations. «رسلنا تَتْرا» (Every society needs a leader. Thought and knowledge alone are not sufficient for humanity; rather, certain matters must be obtained through revelation.)

2- All the Prophets were subjected to denial (or rejection). «كلّما»

3- Every nation had its own Messenger. «كلّما جاء اُمّة رسولها»

4- The result of denying the truth is destruction. «و جعلناهم احادیث» (Nothing remains of the arrogant and the tyrants except history.)

5- Cursing the disbelievers is permissible. «بُعداً» (The word «بُعداً» is a verbal noun (masdar) that is always used in place of a verb. Therefore, «بُعداً» means ba'adū bu'dan [may they be far removed].)

6- The curse in the Quran is due to the disbelief (kufr) of the disbelievers. «لایؤمنون»

7- Historical transformations occur by His will. And the destruction of rebellious nations is a manifestation of His curse and condemnation. «و جعلناهم احادیث فبعداً»

Ayahs

ثُمَّ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ وَأَخَاهُ هَـٰرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (٤٥)
Then We sent Moses and Aaron, his brother, with Our signs and a manifest authority (45)
إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا عَالِينَ (٤٦)
to Pharaoh and his elites; but they acted arrogantly and they were a tyrannical lot. (46)

Key Points

The word "Sultan" is synonymous with hujjah (proof/argument), because a person, by possessing logic, reasoning, and scientific and intellectual proof, becomes dominant and victorious over the hearts of the people.

Messages

1- Propagation (Dawah) must sometimes be carried out by a group of people and involve sending a delegation. «موسى واخاه» (Moses and his brother).

2- The religious propagator must possess scholarly backing. «سلطان مبین» (clear authority/proof).

3- To reform the people and the social system, one must first approach the prominent and influential individuals of society. «الى فرعون و ملائه» (to Pharaoh and his council/chiefs).

4- The associates of the tyrants play an important role in their power. «ملائه»

Ayahs

فَقَالُوٓا۟ أَنُؤْمِنُ لِبَشَرَيْنِ مِثْلِنَا وَقَوْمُهُمَا لَنَا عَـٰبِدُونَ (٤۷)
They said, ‘Shall we believe two humans like ourselves, while their people are our slaves?’ (47)
فَكَذَّبُوهُمَا فَكَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْمُهْلَكِينَ (٤۸)
So they impugned the two of them, whereat they were among those who were destroyed. (48)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ (٤۹)
Certainly We gave Moses the Book so that they might be guided, (49)

Messages

1- Moses (Musa) and Aaron (Harun) were from the lineage of the Children of Israel (Bani Israel). «وقومهما لنا عابدون» (Considering that the Children of Israel had been enslaved by the Pharaohites).

2- Instead of observing logic and miracles, the arrogant focus on the social status of individuals. «سلطان مبین... قومهما لنا عابدون»

3- Racism is a factor leading to arrogance (Istikbar). «وقومهما لنا عابدون» (The Pharaohites considered their own race superior to the Children of Israel).

4- The logic of the prophets is miracles and argumentation, while the logic of the tyrant (Taghut) is the oppression and enslavement of the nation. «قومهما لنا عابدون»

5- The result of denying the truth is destruction. «كذّبوهما فكانوا من المهلكین»

Ayah

وَجَعَلْنَا ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُۥٓ ءَايَةً وَءَاوَيْنَـٰهُمَآ إِلَىٰ رَبْوَةٍ ذَاتِ قَرَارٍ وَمَعِينٍ (٥٠)
and We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign, and sheltered them in a level highland with flowing water. (50)

Key Points

The word ma'īn (مَعین) refers to water that flows upon the earth. Allah has mentioned Jesus and Mary in this Surah as one sign (āyah), but regarding the night and day, He says: «جعلنا اللیل والنّهار آیتَین» [89] (We made the night and the day two signs). This is because the birth of Jesus without a father is the same as Mary's delivery without a husband (i.e., they are two aspects of one miraculous event).

Regarding the elevated region that had flowing water, where Allah settled Jesus and his mother—where is it? Two possibilities have been stated:

A: The intent of rabwah (رَبوَة) is Kufa, the intent of dhāti qarār (ذات قرار) is the Mosque of Kufa, and the intent of ma'īn (مَعین) is the water of the Euphrates, which flows continuously in Kufa. [90]

B: The intent is Damascus, which is one of the best regions of Sham (Syria). [91]

89) Al-Isra, 12. 90) Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 239. 91) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur.

Messages

1- The station of prophethood is the highest of stations. (The name of Jesus is mentioned before his mother due to his station of prophethood). «ابن مریم و اُمّه آیة»

2- Consider important phenomena and strange occurrences as a means of knowing Allah and a sign of His unseen power. «آیة»

3- Water and security are essential advantages for dwelling and life. «ذات قرار و مَعین»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلرُّسُلُ كُلُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ وَٱعْمَلُوا۟ صَـٰلِحًا ۖ إِنِّى بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ (٥۱)
O apostles! Eat of the good things and act righteously. Indeed I know best what you do. (51)

Key Points

In the Holy Quran, alongside the command "كُلوا" (Eat), other instructions are also mentioned, such as: A: "كُلوا... وشكروا" [92] Eat and thank Allah. B: "كُلوا... واطعموا" [93] Eat and feed others. C: "كُلوا... ولاتسرفوا" [94] Eat and do not commit extravagance. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said regarding this Ayah: Prophet Jesus (Isa) was sustained by the earnings from his mother Mary's spinning of thread. [95] Imam Sadiq علیه السلام said regarding this Ayah: The meaning of tayyib (good/pure) is being lawful (halal). [96] We read in a hadith: "اِنّ اللّه لایقبل عبادة مَن فى جُوفه لُقمة من حرام" (Verily, Allah does not accept the worship of one whose stomach contains a morsel of unlawful wealth). If someone has eaten a morsel of unlawful wealth, Allah does not accept his worship. [97]

92) Saba, 15. 93) Hajj, 28. 94) A'raf, 31. 95) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 96) Bihar, Vol. 11, p. 58. 97) Tafsir Maraghi.

Messages

1- Prophets also benefit from foodstuffs and natural bounties. (Unjustified expectations from the Prophets are forbidden.) "كُلوا" (Eat).

2- To invite people to the truth, we must first consider their material life. "كلوا... واعملوا" (Eat... and act).

3- All divine religions oppose monasticism, isolation, and severe asceticism. "كلوا" (Eat). (Perhaps the command to eat, following the mention of Jesus, is to prevent the monasticism that is prevalent in Christianity.)

4- Do not forget two important principles in what you eat: A: Lawfulness (Halal). B: Being tayyib (good) and pure. "كلوا من الطیّبات" (Eat of the good things).

5- Eating food must also be purposeful, not based on whim or desire. "كلوا - واعملوا" (Eat - and act).

6- Purity in the stomach (abstinence/moderation) and piety in action are correlated. "كلوا من الطیّبات واعملوا صالحا" (Eat of the good things and do righteous deeds).

7- The success of righteous action is achieved through lawful (halal) and pure (tayyib) sustenance. "الطیّبات - صالحا" (The good things - righteous).

8- Pure and healthy sustenance and righteous action are the focus of all divine religions. "یا ایّها الرسل - الطیّبات - صالحا" (O Messengers - the good things - righteous).

9- If you eat but do not perform righteous deeds, Allah holds even the Prophets accountable. "اِنّى بما تعملون علیم" (Indeed, I am Knower of what you do).

Ayahs

وَإِنَّ هَـٰذِهِۦٓ أُمَّتُكُمْ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً وَأَنَا۠ رَبُّكُمْ فَٱتَّقُونِ (٥۲)
Indeed this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord, so be wary of Me. (52)
فَتَقَطَّعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَهُم بَيْنَهُمْ زُبُرًا ۖ كُلُّ حِزْبٍۭ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ فَرِحُونَ (٥۳)
But they fragmented their religion among themselves, each party boasting about what it had. (53)

Key Points

The word zubur (زُبُر) is the plural of zubrah (زُبره) (on the pattern of luqmah), meaning a section of the hair on the back of an animal's head that is gathered and separated from the rest; subsequently, this word has been applied to anything that is separated from another thing. [98]

98) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- In the religious worldview and from the divine perspective, all nations are fundamentally one nation, "اُمّة واحدة" (one community), because: A: The principles of the call of all divine Prophets are the same. B: The innate, spiritual, and physical needs of people are the same. C: The Creator and Sustainer of all is one.

2- Piety (taqwa) and maintaining boundaries are worthy of the station of Lordship. "و انَا ربّكم فاتّقون"

3- Creating division is a great affliction that has a long history. "تقطّعوا امرهم"

4- Causing division is a lack of piety. "فاتّقون فتقطّعوا..."

5- The basis of division is human selfishness. "كلّ حزب بمالَدَیهم فَرِحون"

Ayahs

فَذَرْهُمْ فِى غَمْرَتِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ (٥٤)
So leave them in their stupor for a while. (54)
أَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّمَا نُمِدُّهُم بِهِۦ مِن مَّالٍ وَبَنِينَ (٥٥)
Do they suppose that whatever aid We provide them in regard to wealth and children [is because] (55)

Key Points

|

Ayah

نُسَارِعُ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ ۚ بَل لَّا يَشْعُرُونَ (٥٦)
We are eager to bring them good? No, they are not aware! (56)

Key Points

The word ghamrah (غَمرة) means deep water or flood, and what is meant here is the drowning of the people of Mecca in the stagnation and fanaticism of idol worship.

Messages

1- The worst type of deviation is when Allah leaves man to his own devices. (Fadharhum)

2- Disagreement and division are the abyss of destruction and the cause of the wrath and anger of God and the Messenger. (Fataqatta'ū amrahum... fadharhum fī ghamratihim)

3- Man digs the pit of his own misery. (Ghamratihim)

4- Deviants who seek division do not have much time, and Divine wrath awaits them. (Hattā ḥīn)

5- Wealthy, affluent people have an incorrect analysis. Affluence, resources, and male children have caused them to be deceived by themselves and to feel favored by Allah. (Yaḥsabūn...)

6- Evaluation based solely on wealth and children is a sign of ignorance (or lack of perception). (Bal lā yash'urūn)

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ هُم مِّنْ خَشْيَةِ رَبِّهِم مُّشْفِقُونَ (٥۷)
Indeed those who are apprehensive for the fear of their Lord, (57)
وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٥۸)
and believe in the signs of their Lord, (58)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم بِرَبِّهِمْ لَا يُشْرِكُونَ (٥۹)
and do not ascribe partners to their Lord; (59)

Key Points

Khashyah («خَشیت») is a fear that arises from knowledge and recognition, while Ishfaq («اِشفاق») is a fear mixed with love and reverence. Khashyah is primarily related to the heart (internal aspect), while Ishfaq is related to the practical aspect (action). The Ayah states: The believers and those who hasten in good deeds are those in whose hearts fear mixed with the majesty of Allah has taken root, and who, in practice, preserve the sanctity of Allah and possess modesty.

Messages

1- Fear stemming from awareness and attention to the greatness of Allah is a source of growth. «من خَشیة ربّهم مشفقون»

2- Believing in every law that Allah sends down and distancing ourselves from all types of polytheism (Shirk) is our permanent duty. (The words «یؤمنون» and «لایشركون» are present tense verbs, indicating permanence and continuity.)

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْتُونَ مَآ ءَاتَوا۟ وَّقُلُوبُهُمْ وَجِلَةٌ أَنَّهُمْ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ رَٰجِعُونَ (٦٠)
who give whatever they give while their hearts tremble with awe that they are going to return to their Lord (60)

Key Points

Wajil (وَجِل) means anxiety and worry. The spiritual perfection of man occurs in several stages, which are mentioned in these verses:

The first stage: Knowledge and recognition that leads to awe (khashyah). «من خشیة ربّهم» The second stage: Deep and permanent faith in what he has comprehended. «یؤمنون» The third stage: Avoiding all types of polytheism (shirk), hidden and manifest. «لایشركون» The fourth stage: Spending (in charity/infāq) from what Allah has bestowed. «یؤتون...» The fifth stage: Not becoming arrogant and having the concern that perhaps my work is incomplete, or my deed is rejected, or my duty was something else, and I will not be accountable on the Day of Judgment. «قلوبهم وَجِلَة»

Messages

1- We should not become arrogant about our charity (infāq). «یؤتون... و قلوبهم وجلة»

2- The believer's fear of Allah is due to his presence before the Lord on the Day of Resurrection. «وَجلة اَنّهم الى ربّهم راجعون»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُسَـٰرِعُونَ فِى ٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ وَهُمْ لَهَا سَـٰبِقُونَ (٦۱)
—it is they who are zealous in [performing] good works and take the lead in them. (61)

Key Points

In Ayah 56, it was stated that some people consider the mere possession of wealth and children to be the source of happiness and a sign of hastening towards good (khayr). However, this Ayah states: hastening towards good is (achieved through) knowledge, faith, sincerity, and spending (in charity) accompanied by fear and reverence, not what they imagine.

Messages

1- Fear of Allah and the Day of Judgment is the cause of hastening towards good deeds. «من خشیة ربّهم مشفقون... اولئك...»

2- The sign of true faith is constant speed in good deeds. («یسارعون» is a present tense verb, signifying continuity/perpetuity.)

3- Speed and precedence in good deeds is a virtue. «یُسارعون فى الخیرات»

Ayah

وَلَا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۖ وَلَدَيْنَا كِتَـٰبٌ يَنطِقُ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (٦۲)
We task no soul except according to its capacity, and with Us is a book that speaks the truth, and they will not be wronged. (62)

Key Points

In jurisprudence (Fiqh), there is a principle called the rule of "Negation of Hardship and Difficulty" (Qā'idah al-Nafy al-'Usr wa al-Haraj), the basis of which is this very Ayah. This means that whenever an obligation leads to unbearable difficulty, that obligation is nullified.

Messages

1- Although precedence and speed in good deeds are valuable, excessiveness is forbidden. «الاّ وُسعها»

2- The obligations of all human beings are not uniform. Everyone is obligated according to their physical, intellectual, and financial capacity, and Allah does not demand the obligation of the rich from the poor. «لا نكلّف نفساً الاّ وسعها»

3- In the divine worldview, no one suffers from complexes or feelings of inferiority, because they have no obligation beyond their capacity. «الاّ وسعها» (Yes, everyone has a capacity and a duty, so we must have peace of mind.)

4- The system of creation has accountability and record-keeping. «و لَدَینا كتاب»

5- Some conditions for management are mentioned in this Ayah, including:

A: Familiarity with the abilities of individuals and assigning tasks according to their capacity. «وسعها»

B: Precise supervision over their work and duties. «كتاب ینطق بالحقّ»

C: Justice in punishing or rewarding them. «و هم لا یظلمون»

Ayah

بَلْ قُلُوبُهُمْ فِى غَمْرَةٍ مِّنْ هَـٰذَا وَلَهُمْ أَعْمَـٰلٌ مِّن دُونِ ذَٰلِكَ هُمْ لَهَا عَـٰمِلُونَ (٦۳)
Indeed, their hearts are in a stupor in regard to this, and there are other deeds besides which they perpetrate. (63)

Key Points

In the two preceding verses, concerning the sincere wise ones, He said: «هم لها سابقون» (they are foremost in attaining them). In this verse, concerning the deviated group, He says: «هم لها عاملون» (they are workers of them). Indeed, "everyone weaves with his own nature" and "what is in the jug is what pours out of it." The word «بل» (Bal - Nay) at the beginning of the discourse is for transitioning the subject. In the previous verses, the discussion was about the righteous; in this verse, the discussion is about the disbelievers, whose deeds are different from the deeds of the former. The word «غمرة» (Ghamrah) means a deep flood or water that completely covers a person. It is as if heedlessness has encompassed their entire being.

Messages

1- First, the spirit becomes deviated, then the behavior changes. «قلوبهم - لهم اعمال»

2- Worse than ugly behavior is persistence and repetition of it. «لهم اعمال - هم لها عاملون»

3- Man first commits a wrong action, then the wrong action becomes a habit for him and draws him in, meaning he enters the path of committing the deed, and after committing the sin, he becomes its captive. «لهم اعمال - هم لها عاملون»

Ayahs

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَخَذْنَا مُتْرَفِيهِم بِٱلْعَذَابِ إِذَا هُمْ يَجْـَٔرُونَ (٦٤)
When We seize their affluent ones with punishment, behold, they make entreaties [to Us]. (64)
لَا تَجْـَٔرُوا۟ ٱلْيَوْمَ ۖ إِنَّكُم مِّنَّا لَا تُنصَرُونَ (٦٥)
‘Do not make entreaties today! Indeed, you will not receive any help from Us. (65)

Key Points

The word "یَجئَرون" (yaj'arūn) comes from "جوار" (juwār), meaning the howling or wailing of a dog, jackal, or wolf when injured. The comparison of the wailing of the heedless affluent to the howling of a dog is a sign of the contempt and humiliation they face due to their worldly excesses.

Messages

  1. For the affluent and arrogant group, there is no means of awakening other than divine punishment. «حتّى اذا ...»

  2. The end of heedless luxury and blessings will be helpless wailing. «یَجئرون»

After every laugh, there is ultimately a cry; The man who looks to the end (the Hereafter) is a blessed servant.

  1. The Day of Judgment is the day of retribution, not supplication. «لا تَجئروا الیوم»

  2. Those who did not help people with their wealth and resources will not be helped on the Day of Judgment. «لا تنصرون»

Ayahs

قَدْ كَانَتْ ءَايَـٰتِى تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَكُنتُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَعْقَـٰبِكُمْ تَنكِصُونَ (٦٦)
Certainly My signs used to be recited to you, but you used to take to your heels, (66)
مُسْتَكْبِرِينَ بِهِۦ سَـٰمِرًا تَهْجُرُونَ (٦۷)
being disdainful of it, talking nonsense in your nightly sessions.’ (67)

Key Points

Nukūs (نكوص) means returning backward, and ‘alā a‘qābikum (على اَعقابكم) emphasizes this. Sāmir (سامر) is derived from samar (سَمر), meaning nocturnal conversation. Hajr (هَجر) means separation, and hujr (هُجر) refers to abuse and cursing, which causes separation.

Messages

1- Regression and turning away from the truth are characteristics of the affluent and the arrogant. «فكنتم على اعقابكم تنكصون»

2- Disregard for the prophets is not a sign of growth and progress; rather, it is a factor leading to downfall and regression. Divine commands and laws are the means to achieve perfection, and turning away from them leads to downfall. «اعقابكم تنكصون»

3- Worse than regression and turning away from the truth is when the purpose of this action is arrogance (istakbār). «مستكبرین به»

4- He who lacks logic during the day engages in nonsense at night. «سامراً تَهجُرون»

5- He who has no reasonable argument resorts to cursing. «تَهجُرون»

Ayahs

أَفَلَمْ يَدَّبَّرُوا۟ ٱلْقَوْلَ أَمْ جَآءَهُم مَّا لَمْ يَأْتِ ءَابَآءَهُمُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (٦۸)
Have they not contemplated the Discourse, or has anything come to them [in it] that did not come to their forefathers? (68)
أَمْ لَمْ يَعْرِفُوا۟ رَسُولَهُمْ فَهُمْ لَهُۥ مُنكِرُونَ (٦۹)
Is it that they do not recognize their apostle, and so they deny him? (69)

Key Points

|

Ayah

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ بِهِۦ جِنَّةٌۢ ۚ بَلْ جَآءَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ لِلْحَقِّ كَـٰرِهُونَ (۷٠)
Do they say, ‘There is madness in him’? No, he has brought them the truth, and most of them are averse to the truth. (70)

Messages

1- The primary cause of people's misery is the suspension of thought and reflection. «أفلم یدّبّروا القول»

2- The Quran is a book of contemplation and reflection. (Not merely recitation and tajwid, etc.; whoever reflects upon it understands its truthfulness.) «أفلم یدّبّروا القول»

3- Allah has conveyed His call to people throughout all ages, and the foundation of the heavenly religions is one. «ام جائهم ما لم یأت»

4- In propagation and invitation, block all avenues of excuse-making for the opponents of the truth. «ام جائهم ما لم یأت», «ام لم یعرفوا»

5- The Prophet must be (well) known among the people so that there is no excuse for denial. «ام لم یعرفوا رسولهم»

6- Although man reacts to new speech, the revelation of the Divine message is not new. «ام جائهم ما لم یأت آبائهم الاوّلین»

7- The enemies of religion, in order to preserve their doctrine, damage the character of the men of God. «ام یقولون به جِنّة»

8- The truth must be presented, even if the majority are displeased. «بل جاءهم بالحقّ و اكثرهم للحقّ كارهون»

9- Some disbelievers possess a sound nature (fitrah) and accept the truth. «اكثرهم للحقّ كارهون»

Ayah

وَلَوِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلْحَقُّ أَهْوَآءَهُمْ لَفَسَدَتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ وَمَن فِيهِنَّ ۚ بَلْ أَتَيْنَـٰهُم بِذِكْرِهِمْ فَهُمْ عَن ذِكْرِهِم مُّعْرِضُونَ (۷۱)
Had the truth followed their desires, the heavens and the earth would have surely fallen apart [along] with those who are in them. Indeed, We have brought them their Reminder, but they are disregardful of their Reminder. (71)

Key Points

If the Truth (Haqq) were to follow the desires (hawā) of the people, the cosmic order would become corrupt and ruined, because: 1- The desires of people—even the desires of a single person—are contradictory at different times. 2- The desires of people lead to corruption (mafsadah). 3- The demands of the people are one-dimensional and do not pay attention to other dimensions or to distant and immediate consequences.

Messages

1- The Truth (Haqq) must never be subject to the inclinations and demands of the people. «ولو اتّبع»

2- The disbelieving elite wish that religion would conform to their desires. «ولو اتّبع الحقّ»

3- Worshipping the Truth is in conflict with worshipping desires. «لفسدت»

4- Human inclinations and desires corrupt the world. «لفسدت»

5- The existing order in the universe is a sign of the Truthfulness and Wisdom of the One God (Allah). «و لو اتّبع الحقّ... لفسدت»

6- There are living and conscious beings in the heavens. «مَن فیهنّ»

7- The Quran is a means of remembrance/admonition. «بذكرهم»

8- Religion is a means for the awakening and honor of nations and their salvation from destruction. «بذكرهم»

9- Sometimes man turns away from paying attention to that which is the cause of his honor and good name. «فهم عن ذكرهم معرضون»

Ayahs

أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ خَرْجًا فَخَرَاجُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ (۷۲)
Do you ask a recompense from them? Your Lord’s recompense is better, and He is the best of providers. (72)
وَإِنَّكَ لَتَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۷۳)
Indeed you invite them to a straight path, (73)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَإِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ عَنِ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ لَنَـٰكِبُونَ (۷٤)
and those who do not believe in the Hereafter surely deviate from the path. (74)

Key Points

Kharij (خرج) is something that is spent (consumed), but Kharaj (خراج) is a continuous budget that is determined. [99] The word nakb (نكب) means deviation, and nakbat (نكبت) means that the world has turned its back on him and he has become wretched. Since divine sustenance is good, "خراج ربّك خیرٌ" (Kharaj of your Lord is better) has several advantages: it is without obligation (or reproach), permanent, abundant, and blessed. We read in a Hadith: Those who turn to others instead of the Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and accept their leadership are deviating from the straight path. [100]

99) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran. 100) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- In propagating the religion, one should not demand money from the people; if they give it themselves, that is a separate matter. "ام تسئلهم"

2- People are sensitive to the preacher asking for money. "ام تسئلهم خرجا"

3- People give a one-time expenditure (kharij), but Allah always provides sustenance. "خرج - خراج" (Considering the difference explained between kharij and kharaj)

4- Providing continuous sustenance is among the prerogatives of the Lord. "فخراج ربّك"

5- Allah guarantees the sustenance of the propagators of the religion. "فخراج ربّك"

6- Do not be deceived by the intermediaries of sustenance. "وهو خیرالرّازقین"

7- The Prophet is the caller to the correct path. "انّك لتدعوهم الى صراط مستقیم"

8- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the factor for steadfastness on the straight path, and lack of belief is the factor for deviation. "لا یؤمنون - لناكبون"

Ayah

۞ وَلَوْ رَحِمْنَـٰهُمْ وَكَشَفْنَا مَا بِهِم مِّن ضُرٍّ لَّلَجُّوا۟ فِى طُغْيَـٰنِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (۷٥)
Should We have mercy upon them and remove their distress, they would surely persist, bewildered in their rebellion. (75)

Key Points

We read in the Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq and Rūḥ al-Bayān: Once, a famine struck Mecca. Abū Sufyān requested the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله to pray. The Prophet raised His hands in supplication, and the famine was lifted. Then the above Ayah was revealed.

Transgression (ṭughyān) differs among various individuals: The transgression of scholars is in knowledge, which manifests through boasting and pride. The transgression of the wealthy is in wealth, which reveals itself through stinginess. The transgression of the righteous is in good deeds, which becomes apparent through riyā' (showing off) and sum'ah (self-display and seeking fame). The transgression of those who follow their desires manifests in the pursuit of lusts. [101]

101) Tafsir Rūḥ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Prosperity and comfort are manifestations of Divine Mercy. «رَحِمناهم و كشفنا»

2- Service and kindness shown to unprincipled individuals are fruitless. «رَحِمنا - للجّوا»

3- Faithless people misuse opportunities and respites. «لایؤمنون بالآخرة... للجّوا فى طغیانهم»

4- Transgression causes man to wander aimlessly. «فى طغیانهم یعمهون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَخَذْنَـٰهُم بِٱلْعَذَابِ فَمَا ٱسْتَكَانُوا۟ لِرَبِّهِمْ وَمَا يَتَضَرَّعُونَ (۷٦)
We have already seized them with punishment, yet they did not humble themselves before their Lord, nor will they entreat [Him for mercy] (76)

Key Points

We read in the Hadith: Istikānah (اِستكانت) is humility (khuḍūʿ), and taḍarruʿ (تضرّع) is raising the hands in supplication. (102) In the previous Ayah, He said concerning the stubborn disbelievers: If We show them mercy, they persist in stubbornness and do not come to the right path; in this Ayah, He says: If We seize them with Our wrath and punish them, they still do not come to the right path. The poet says: What good is preaching to the black-hearted? An iron nail will not penetrate stone.

102) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The purpose of Divine wrath in this world is to awaken the heedless. «اخذناهم ... فما استكانوا»

2- Sometimes disbelievers and the misguided are afflicted by Divine punishments even in this world. «اخذناهم»

3- Stubborn disbelievers are guided neither by Allah's mercy nor by His wrath and punishment. «فما استكانوا»

4- The worst type of arrogance is pride against the Lord (Rabb). «فما استكانوا لربّهم» (A hard-hearted person reaches a point where he never shows humility, supplication, or lamentation before the Great God.)

Ayah

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِم بَابًا ذَا عَذَابٍ شَدِيدٍ إِذَا هُمْ فِيهِ مُبْلِسُونَ (۷۷)
until We open on them the gate of a severe punishment, whereupon they will be despondent in it. (77)

Key Points

| The term Mublis (مُبلس), derived from the root Iblās (اِبلاس), means the grief that overtakes a person following a bitter and severe incident, often leading him to silence, bewilderment, and despair. [103]

103) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- The doors of punishment are initially closed to us, but our stubbornness and obstinacy against the truth cause Allah to open those doors. "فتحنا"

2- Allah implements principles in the stages of nurturing (discipline):

First: He deals with mercy and kindness. "ولو رَحِمناهم وكشفنا ما بهم من ضرّ للجوّا"

Second: With punitive punishment. "و لقد اخذناهم بالعذاب فما استكانوا"

Third: With the final punishment, which is the source of helplessness and distress. "عذابٍ شدیدٍ"

3- Whoever seeks, through obstinacy, to drive the Prophets to despair, will be afflicted by a wrath that causes him to be driven to despair himself. "مبلسون"

4- In the final punishment, there is no room for repentance or salvation, and those being punished become despairing. "مبلسون"

Ayahs

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنشَأَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةَ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ (۷۸)
It is He who made for you hearing, eyesight, and hearts. Little do you thank. (78)
وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى ذَرَأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (۷۹)
It is He who created you on the earth, and you will be mustered toward Him. (79)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ وَلَهُ ٱخْتِلَـٰفُ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (۸٠)
And it is He who gives life and brings death, and due to Him is the alternation of day and night. Do you not exercise your reason? (80)

Messages

1- The means of knowledge are among the greatest Divine blessings. «اَنشأ لكم السمع...»

2- The path to knowledge is not limited to sensory perceptions; rather, the heart (or intellect) is also a tool for recognition. «السمع و الافئدة»

3- Gratitude towards the Benefactor is obligatory, and whoever does not utilize the blessings properly is ungrateful and subject to reproach. «قلیلاً ما تشكرون»

4- Remembering Divine blessings is the foundation for recognition and gratitude. «تشكرون»

5- The creation of man is purposeful. «ذرأكم - و الیه تحشرون»

6- Thought and intellect lead man to Monotheism (Tawhid). «و هو الّذى... أفلاتعقلون»

Ayahs

بَلْ قَالُوا۟ مِثْلَ مَا قَالَ ٱلْأَوَّلُونَ (۸۱)
Indeed, they say, just like what the former peoples said. (81)
قَالُوٓا۟ أَءِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًا وَعِظَـٰمًا أَءِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ (۸۲)
They said, ‘What, when we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we be resurrected? (82)

Key Points

|

Ayah

لَقَدْ وُعِدْنَا نَحْنُ وَءَابَآؤُنَا هَـٰذَا مِن قَبْلُ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۸۳)
Certainly, we and our fathers were promised this before. [But] these are nothing but myths of the ancients.’ (83)

Key Points

Asatir (اساطیر) is the plural of usturah (اسطوره) and means false legends. This word has been used nine times in the Quran, spoken by the disbelievers against the prophets.

Messages

1- The disbelievers have nothing new to say regarding the denial of the Resurrection. «مثل ما قال الاوّلون»

2- Disbelievers lack logic and reasoning, and their only weapon against the truth is astonishment coupled with denial. «ءَاِذا»

3- Lack of intellect (reasoning) is the root of denying the truth. (Attention to death and life and the rotation of night and day prepares the ground for faith in the Resurrection.) «یحیى ویمیت... أفلاتعقلون... قالوا ءاذا مِتنا»

Ayahs

قُل لِّمَنِ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَمَن فِيهَآ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۸٤)
Say, ‘To whom does the earth belong and whoever it contains, if you know?’ (84)
سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِ ۚ قُلْ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ (۸٥)
They will say, ‘To Allah.’ Say, ‘Will you not then take admonition?’ (85)

Key Points

|

Ayahs

قُلْ مَن رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ ٱلسَّبْعِ وَرَبُّ ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۸٦)
Say, ‘Who is the Lord of the seven heavens and the Lord of the Great Throne?’ (86)
سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِ ۚ قُلْ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۸۷)
They will say, ‘[They belong] to Allah.’ Say, ‘Will you not then be wary [of Him]?’ (87)

Key Points

The term «ربّ» (Rabb) refers to an Owner who is intent on preserving and managing His dominion. The «عرش» (Throne) is the center of Allah’s command and is distinct from the seven heavens.

Messages

1- The root of polytheism (Shirk) is ignorance and heedlessness. «لمن الارض... اِن كنتم تعلمون»

2- One of the methods of propagation is posing questions. «لمَن الارض»

3- Beneficial knowledge is that which leads man to know Allah. «لمَن الارض... ان كنتم تعلمون»

4- Every aware person knows that the earth and all those who are in it are not without an Owner or without accountability. «سیقولون لله»

5- Conscience is the best judge. «سیقولون لله»

6- Prepare evidence for admonition and argumentation from the sayings and answers of the people themselves. «قل أفلاتذكّرون»

7- The seven heavens and the entire existence are under His management. «ربّ السموات ...»

Ayahs

قُلْ مَنۢ بِيَدِهِۦ مَلَكُوتُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَهُوَ يُجِيرُ وَلَا يُجَارُ عَلَيْهِ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۸۸)
Say, ‘In whose hand is the dominion of all things, and who gives shelter and no shelter can be provided from Him, if you know?’ (88)
سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِ ۚ قُلْ فَأَنَّىٰ تُسْحَرُونَ (۸۹)
They will say, ‘[They all belong] to Allah.’ Say, ‘Then how are you so deluded?’ (89)

Key Points

|

Ayah

بَلْ أَتَيْنَـٰهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (۹٠)
Indeed, We have brought them the truth, and they are indeed liars. (90)

Key Points

The Quran states in Surah Ya Sin: «انّما أمره اذا اراد شیئاً اَن یَقول له كُن فیكون فسُبحان الّذى بیده ملكوت كلّ شى ء» [104] Whenever Allah wills a thing, He commands it, and it is created; thus, exalted is Allah, in Whose Hand is the dominion (Malakūt) of everything. (Therefore, the word Malakūt means absolute power over existence.) The words «یجیر» (Yujīr) and «یجار» (Yujār) stem from the root Jiwār, meaning neighborliness (or proximity). Since one of the rights of neighborliness is protecting the neighbor against the malicious intent of others, this word is used to mean granting refuge and protection. [105] The meaning of Al-Haqq (the Truth) in the Ayah «اَتیناهم بالحقّ» (We brought them the Truth) is either the Resurrection (Ma'ād), or the Quran, or the logic and monotheistic reasoning.

104) Ya Sin, 82 to 83. 105) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Let us learn the method of dialogue with opponents from the Quran. «قل» (Say)

2- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), let us utilize people's sound innate nature (Fitrah) and correct beliefs for their growth and guidance. «قل مَن بیده ...» (Say: Who is it in Whose Hand...)

3- Allah's dominion (Mālikiyyah) is vast and comprehensive. «ملكوت كلّ شى ء» (the dominion of everything)

4- Allah's granting of refuge is permanent, absolute, and exclusive. «و هو یجیر» (and He grants refuge)

5- There is no refuge against the wrath of Allah. «و لا یُجار علیه» (and against Him no refuge is granted)

6- Only the knowledgeable understand the absolute sovereignty of Allah. «ان كنتم تعلمون» (if you know)

7- Take a clear stance against the deviant beliefs of others. «بل اَتیناهم بالحقّ» (Nay, We brought them the Truth)

8- The actions of Allah are truthful (Haqq) and wise. «بالحقّ» (with the Truth)

Ayah

مَا ٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ مِن وَلَدٍ وَمَا كَانَ مَعَهُۥ مِنْ إِلَـٰهٍ ۚ إِذًا لَّذَهَبَ كُلُّ إِلَـٰهٍۭ بِمَا خَلَقَ وَلَعَلَا بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (۹۱)
Allah has not taken any offspring, neither is there any god besides Him, for then each god would take away what he created, and some of them would surely rise up against others. Clear is Allah of what they allege! (91)

Messages

1- Belief in any kind of offspring for Allah is forbidden. (Whether we consider Jesus His son, or the angels, or others.) «مِن وَلَدٍ»

2- Every creature is under the dominion of the Creator. «لَذَهَبَ كُلُّ إِلَٰهٍ بِمَا خَلَقَ»

3- Multiplicity of management prevents success. «لَذَهَبَ كُلُّ إِلَٰهٍ بِمَا خَلَقَ»

4- The harmonious and well-ordered system is a sign of Allah's Oneness. «إِذًا لَّذَهَبَ كُلُّ إِلَٰهٍ»

5- Having offspring and partners is a sign of need and limitation, and the necessity of limitation is the seeking of superiority. «لَذَهَبَ - لَعَلَا»

6- Conflict between powers is a cause of corruption and instability. «لَعَلَا بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ»

7- Whenever we recount the false and deviant notions of others, we should glorify Allah (make tasbih) to purify our own souls and the souls of others. «سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ»

8- We must consider Allah far removed and purified from all deviations and superstitions. «عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ»

Ayahs

عَـٰلِمِ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ فَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (۹۲)
The Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, He is above having any partners that they ascribe [to Him]. (92)
قُل رَّبِّ إِمَّا تُرِيَنِّى مَا يُوعَدُونَ (۹۳)
Say, ‘My Lord! If You should show me what they are promised, (93)

Key Points

|

Ayah

رَبِّ فَلَا تَجْعَلْنِى فِى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۹٤)
then do not put me, my Lord, among the wrongdoing lot.’ (94)

Key Points

| Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding the phrase «عالم الغیب و الشهادة»: The Unseen (Ghayb) is that which has not yet occurred, and the Seen (Shahadah) is that which has been realized. [106]

106) Ma'ani al-Akhbar, p. 146.

Messages

1- Allah's knowledge of everything is the secret to His independence from having a partner. «ما كان معه من اله... عالم الغیب»

2- Allah's knowledge regarding the Unseen (Ghayb) and the Seen (Shahadah) is equal. «عالم الغیب و الشهادة»

3- Awareness of the Unseen is more important, and the matters of the Unseen are more numerous. (Throughout the entire Qur'an, the word Ghayb precedes Shahadah). «عالم الغیب و الشهادة»

4- The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is the trustee of revelation. He even conveys the word «قل» (Say).

5- Presence among the oppressors entails the danger of being afflicted by divine punishments. «فى القوم الظالمین»

Ayah

وَإِنَّا عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّرِيَكَ مَا نَعِدُهُمْ لَقَـٰدِرُونَ (۹٥)
We are indeed able to show you what We promise them. (95)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah consoles His Prophet, assuring him that He has the power to punish the misguided and the disbelievers, but He delays their punishment for several reasons, including: A: He grants them respite so they may repent. B: To complete the argument (proof) against them. C: Believing individuals will emerge from their progeny in the future. D: Due to the presence of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, who is a source of mercy and blessing.

Messages

1- The delay in punishment is not a sign of Allah's weakness. «لقادرون»

2- Do not doubt Allah's power just because you do not witness the fate of the disbelievers with your own eyes. «نریك ما نعدهم لقارون»

Ayahs

ٱدْفَعْ بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ ٱلسَّيِّئَةَ ۚ نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَصِفُونَ (۹٦)
Repel ill [conduct] with that which is the best. We know best whatever they allege. (96)
وَقُل رَّبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ هَمَزَٰتِ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينِ (۹۷)
Say, ‘My Lord! I seek Your protection from the promptings of devils, (97)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ رَبِّ أَن يَحْضُرُونِ (۹۸)
and I seek Your protection, my Lord, from their presence near me.’ (98)

Messages

1- The Noble Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) is under the tutelage of Allah and is commanded to respond to the enemies' evil deeds in the best possible manner. «اِدفع بالّتى هِىَ اَحسن»

2- Evil can be answered with evil, but this is not appropriate for a worthy leader. «ادفع بالّتى هىَ اَحسن»

3- Our faith in Allah's knowledge is the factor that generates patience and forbearance within us. «نحن اعلم»

4- Granting refuge is among the attributes of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّ اَعوذبك»

5- Even the Prophets must seek refuge in Allah. «قل ربّ اَعوذبك»

6- Seeking refuge in Allah must be frequent and serious. (Repetition of «اعوذ»)

7- The only reliable refuge is Allah. «بك»

8- One of the ways Satan infiltrates is through his suggestions and whisperings regarding our bad interactions with others. «هَمزات»

9- The devils (Shayāṭīn) are numerous, and their whisperings are also varied. «هَمَزات الشیاطین»

10- The devils also covet the Prophets. «هَمَزات الشیاطین»

11- Higher than mere whispering is the presence of the devils. «اَن یَحضُرون»

12- The presence of corrupt and corrupting individuals in society is a prelude to the corruption of others. «یَحضُرون»

Ayahs

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَ أَحَدَهُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ قَالَ رَبِّ ٱرْجِعُونِ (۹۹)
When death comes to one of them, he says, ‘My Lord! Take me back, (99)
لَعَلِّىٓ أَعْمَلُ صَـٰلِحًا فِيمَا تَرَكْتُ ۚ كَلَّآ ۚ إِنَّهَا كَلِمَةٌ هُوَ قَآئِلُهَا ۖ وَمِن وَرَآئِهِم بَرْزَخٌ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ (۱٠٠)
that I may act righteously in what I have left behind.’ ‘By no means! These are mere words that he says.’ And before them is a barrier until the day they will be resurrected. (100)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Arrogance and heedlessness are permanent for a group (of people). «حتّى اذا»

2- The deviated will awaken one day and request return to the world, but that request is unattainable. «ربّ ارجعون»

3- Wasting life and opportunity causes regret at the time of death. «ربّ ارجعون»

4- The disbelievers' request for return is serious, but their promise regarding becoming righteous is doubtful. «لعلّى اعمل»

5- Righteous action (عمل صالح) is the provision for after death. «ارجعون لعلّى اعمل صالحا»

6- Those who possess wealth and means must perform more righteous actions. «اعمل صالحا فیما تركت»

O you whose hand has the power, do a deed, before no deed can come from you.

7- The world is the farm of the Hereafter. «اعمل صالحا فیما تركت»

8- The confession of error by the disbelievers is only verbal and lacks heartfelt support. «انّها كلمة هوقائلها»

Ayahs

فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَلَآ أَنسَابَ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَلَا يَتَسَآءَلُونَ (۱٠۱)
When the Trumpet is blown, there will be no ties between them on that day, nor will they ask [about] each other. (101)
فَمَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۱٠۲)
Then those whose deeds weigh heavy in the scales—it is they who are the felicitous. (102)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ فِى جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدُونَ (۱٠۳)
As for those whose deeds weigh light in the scales—they will be the ones who have ruined their souls, and they will remain in hell [forever]. (103)

Key Points

| Question: In some verses, it is stated that people will question one another on the Day of Judgment, «واَقبل بعضهم على بعض یَتسائلون» (107), or the people of Paradise will ask the inhabitants of Hell: What caused you to enter Hell? «ما سَلكَكم فى سقر» (108). So why does the verse under discussion state that people will not question one another «و لا یتسائلون»? What is the reason for this difference? Answer: These two points do not contradict each other. The first case, where He says, "people will question one another," relates to the time after the reckoning (Hisab wa Kitab) has been settled, and it concerns the people of Paradise and Hell who ask things of each other. However, the verse under discussion relates to the beginning of the Resurrection, the time of reckoning, and the stage before people enter Paradise or Hell, where He states that they will not question one another. (109)

107) Saffat, 27; Tur, 25. 108) Muddaththir, 42. 109) Al-Mizan, Vol. 15, p. 69.

Messages

  1. The beginning of the Resurrection is with the blowing of the Trumpet (Nafkh as-Sur). «فاذا نفخ... فلا انساب»
  2. On the Day of Judgment, worldly sources of pride are erased. «فلا انساب بینهم»
  3. All worldly deeds are subject to reckoning and accounting. «فمَن ثَقُلت - و مَن خَفّت»
  4. There is a scale (Mizan) for every deed. «مَوازینه»
  5. The greatest loss for man is wasting his life and talents. «خسروا انفسهم»
