  6. The successful (Muflihun) are those who have stored provisions for the Day of Judgment. «هم المفلحون»

Ayahs

تَلْفَحُ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ وَهُمْ فِيهَا كَـٰلِحُونَ (۱٠٤)
The Fire will scorch their faces, while they snarl baring their teeth. (104)
أَلَمْ تَكُنْ ءَايَـٰتِى تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَكُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ (۱٠٥)
‘Was it not that My signs were recited to you but you would deny them?’ (105)

Key Points

The word lafḥ (لفح) means burning the face, and the word kaliḥ (كلح) means the retraction of the lip and the exposure of the teeth.

Messages

1- The Fire continuously and constantly burns the faces of the inhabitants of Hell. (Talfaḥ (تَلفَح) is a present tense verb (فعل مضارع) and signifies continuity.)

2- The disbelievers in Hell suffer both physical torment, "تَلفَح وجوههم" (their faces are scorched), and spiritual torment and rebuke. "ألم تكن ..." (Were not...).

3- The alteration of the face by the Fire leads to the humiliation of the sinners. "كالِحون" (grimacing/disfigured).

4- The punishment of Allah follows the completion of the argument (proof). "تَلفح وجوههم... ألم تكن آیاتى تُتلى" (Their faces are scorched... Were not My verses recited).

5- Persistence in denial (or rejection) is the most fundamental reason for entering Hell. "فكنتم بها تكذّبون" (and you used to deny them).

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا غَلَبَتْ عَلَيْنَا شِقْوَتُنَا وَكُنَّا قَوْمًا ضَآلِّينَ (۱٠٦)
They will say, ‘Our Lord! Our wretchedness overcame us, and we were an astray lot. (106)
رَبَّنَآ أَخْرِجْنَا مِنْهَا فَإِنْ عُدْنَا فَإِنَّا ظَـٰلِمُونَ (۱٠۷)
Our Lord! Bring us out of this! Then, if we revert [to our previous conduct], we will indeed be wrongdoers.’ (107)

Key Points

|

Ayah

قَالَ ٱخْسَـُٔوا۟ فِيهَا وَلَا تُكَلِّمُونِ (۱٠۸)
He will say, ‘Begone in it, and do not speak to Me! (108)

Key Points

The word khasā (خسأ) means to be driven away accompanied by insult, which is typically said to a dog. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Do not sit with anyone who drinks wine, for they are the dogs of Hellfire. Then He recited this Ayah: [110] «قال اخسئوا فیها...» It is mentioned in a hadith that the faces of the inhabitants of Hell, after hearing ikhsa'ū (اخسئوا), will turn into a piece of flesh that merely breathes. [111] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The inhabitants of Hell became wretched due to their deeds. [112]

110) Bihar, Vol. 76, p. 147 111) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 112) Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 356.

Messages

1- Consciences will awaken on the Day of Judgment, and the criminals will humbly confess their sins. «غَلَبت علینا شِقوتنا»

2- Deviation from the path of the Prophets is the cause of wretchedness and misery. «آیاتى تُتلى، غَلَبت علینا شِقوتنا»

3- On the Day of Judgment, Allah will speak to the disbelievers using the most crushing words. «اخسئوا فیها و لاتكلّمون»

Ayah

إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْ عِبَادِى يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَٱرْحَمْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ (۱٠۹)
Indeed there was a part of My servants who would say, ‘‘Our Lord! We have believed. So forgive us, and have mercy on us, and You are the best of the merciful.’’ (109)

Messages

1- Allah does not leave the verbal abuse (slander/taunts) directed by the deviants towards the believers unanswered. «انّه كان...»

2- Supplication to Allah in this world is beneficial, «كان فریق من عبادى یقولون ربّنا...» but supplication and pleading on the Day of Resurrection will not be beneficial. «ربّنا اخرجنا... قال اخسئوا»

3- The servants of Allah are always fearful of their shortcomings and seek forgiveness. «كان... یقولون ربّنا...»

4- Faith is the prerequisite for forgiveness, and forgiveness is the prerequisite for receiving Divine Mercy. «آمنّا فاغفرلنا وارحمنا»

5- Allah is the source of all mercies. «و انت خیرالرّاحمین»

6- Among the etiquette of supplication is glorifying Allah. «و انت خیرالرّاحمین»

Ayah

فَٱتَّخَذْتُمُوهُمْ سِخْرِيًّا حَتَّىٰٓ أَنسَوْكُمْ ذِكْرِى وَكُنتُم مِّنْهُمْ تَضْحَكُونَ (۱۱٠)
But you took them by ridicule until they made you forget My remembrance, and you used to laugh at them. (110)

Key Points

There is a great difference between sukhriyyan (سُخریّا) and sikhriyyan (سِخریّا). If relationships in a society are based on empathy, cooperation, and service, and all individuals benefit from each other's skills and innovation, the society grows, is strengthened, and all affairs are organized. The noble Ayah, «لیتّخذ بعضهم بعضا سُخریّا» (113), carries this message. However, if relationships between individuals are based on humiliation, weakening, cursing, and discouragement, such a society will lead to the forgetting of Allah and downfall. The Ayah under discussion reflects this meaning: «فاتّخذتموهم سخریّا». The fact that the believers are considered the cause of the disbelievers' heedlessness, «انسوكم ذكرى», might be because if the believers had not existed, the reality of the disbelievers' hostility toward the truth would not have become manifest. (114)

113) Az-Zukhruf, 32. 114) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- The punishment for insulting is being insulted. (Allah supports the believers and responds to the insult directed at them.) «فاتّخذتموهم سخریّا - اخسئوا فیها»

2- Disregard for the rituals of prayer and supplication of Allah’s servants leads to Allah’s disregard. «سخریّا - ولا تكلّمون»

3- Believers are constantly exposed to the ridicule of the disbelievers. «و كنتم منهم تضحكون»

4- The punishment for ridiculing the believers is forgetting the remembrance of Allah. «انسوكم ذكرى»

5- Contemptuous laughter is among the major sins (al-Kaba'ir). (115) «و كنتم منهم تضحكون» (Ordinary laughter is separate from arrogant laughter.)

115) Any sin that is followed by a promise of punishment is considered a major sin (al-Kaba'ir). Since Hellfire is mentioned for contemptuous laughter directed at the believers, it is therefore a major sin.

Ayahs

إِنِّى جَزَيْتُهُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ بِمَا صَبَرُوٓا۟ أَنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ (۱۱۱)
Indeed I have rewarded them today for their patience. They are indeed the triumphant.’ (111)
قَـٰلَ كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ عَدَدَ سِنِينَ (۱۱۲)
He will say, ‘How many years did you remain on earth?’ (112)

Key Points

|

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ فَسْـَٔلِ ٱلْعَآدِّينَ (۱۱۳)
They will say, ‘We remained for a day, or part of a day; yet ask those who keep the count.’ (113)
قَـٰلَ إِن لَّبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا ۖ لَّوْ أَنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۱۱٤)
He will say, ‘You only remained a little; if only you had known. (114)

Key Points

The word "fawz" (فوز) means reaching the goal/objective. On the Day of Judgment, people will be asked: How long did you reside in the world? «كم لبثتم» (How long did you remain?) This question is raised several times in the Qur'an, and everyone answers according to their own perception, including: We remained in the world for the duration of an hour of a day. «ساعة من نهار» [116] An evening, or a morning. «عشیّة اوضحاها» [117] A day or part of a day. «یوماً او بعض یوم» [118] It is possible that the intent of «كم لبثتم فى الارض» (How long did you remain on the earth?) is the duration of the stay in the grave and the intermediate world (Barzakh).

116) Al-Ahqaf, 35. 117) An-Nazi'at, 46. 118) Al-Mu'minun, 113.

Messages

1- The reward of the patient ones is from Allah Himself. «اِنّى جزیتهم» (Patience in the face of harassment, insults, and mockery carries a great reward.)

2- Salvation (Fawz) is achieved through steadfastness. «اَنّهم هم الفائزون»

3- On the Day of Judgment, the only successful group are the patient ones. [119] «اَنّهم هم الفائزون»

Patience and victory are both old friends; victory comes after patience.

4- The great regret is that a person purchases Hell and eternal torment for the sake of a few days of pleasure and comfort. «لبثنا یوماً» (We remained a day.)

5- Do not be deceived by the respite of time, for it is a very short duration. «یَوماً اوبعض یوم»

6- The entire lifespan of the world is insignificant compared to eternity. «یوماً او بَعض یوم»

7- Some angels are tasked with counting and keeping statistics of the days. «فسئل العادّین» (So ask the counters.)

119) The pronoun «هم» (they/them) alongside «انّهم» (indeed they), points to the exclusivity of salvation (Fawz) to that group.

Ayah

أَفَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنَّمَا خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ عَبَثًا وَأَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْنَا لَا تُرْجَعُونَ (۱۱٥)
Did you suppose that We created you aimlessly, and that you will not be brought back to Us?’ (115)

Key Points

The Quran has stated goals for the creation of humanity, including: 1- Worship. «و ما خلقت الجنّ والانس الاّ لیعبدون» (120) I did not create the jinn and mankind except that they should worship Me (and thereby achieve perfection and draw near to Me). 2- Trial/Test. «خَلَق الموت والحیاة لیَبلوكم ایّكم احسن عملاً» (121) (Allah) created death and life to test you, [to see] which of you is best in deed. 3- To receive Divine Mercy. «الاّ مَن رحم ربّك و لذلك خلقهم» (122) Except those upon whom your Lord has mercy, and for that (mercy) He created them. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «رحم اللّه امرء عرف قدره» May Allah have mercy upon a person who knows his own worth and standing. (123) And knows where he came from, where he is now, and where he will eventually go. Imam Husayn عليه السلام said: «انّ اللّه ما خلق العباد الاّ لیعرفوه فاذا عرفوه عبدوه فاذا عبدوه استغنوا بعبادته عن عبادة من سواه» The Exalted Allah did not create His servants except that they should know Him. When they know Him, they worship Him, and when they worship God, through His worship they become independent of the worship of anyone other than Him. (124) Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «الدّنیا خُلقت لغیرها ولم تخلق لنفسها» (125) The world was created for something else and was not created for itself. (The world is a means to reach the Hereafter.) Indeed, those who seek all their goals within this world become earth-oriented (materialistic) and stuck to the earth. As stated in Ayah 176 of Surah Al-A'raf: «اخلد الى الارض» Every moment of precious life is an irreplaceable treasure that passes away. A treasure like this is wasted every moment, alas, alas!

120) Adh-Dhariyat, 56. 121) Al-Mulk, 2. 122) Hud, 119. 123) Ghurar al-Hikam. 124) Bihar, Vol. 5, p. 312. 125) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 463.

Messages

1- The creation of humanity is purposeful. «أفحسبتم انّما خلقناكم عبثا»

2- Not all human calculations are realistic. «أفحَسِبتم»

3- Folly and futility have no place in Allah's work. «أفحَسِبتم... عَبَثا»

4- Worldly life without the Hereafter is futile and void. «عَبَثا»

5- Humanity is responsible and committed. (We must prepare ourselves for accountability on the Day of Judgment). «أفحسبتم ... لا ترجعون»

6- The goal of human creation is not confined to this world. «أفحسبتم...لاترجعون»

Ayahs

فَتَعَـٰلَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلْكَرِيمِ (۱۱٦)
So exalted is Allah, the True Sovereign, there is no god except Him, the Lord of the Noble Throne. (116)
وَمَن يَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ لَا بُرْهَـٰنَ لَهُۥ بِهِۦ فَإِنَّمَا حِسَابُهُۥ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۱۱۷)
Whoever invokes besides Allah another god of which he has no proof, his reckoning will indeed rest with his Lord. Indeed the faithless will not be felicitous. (117)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَقُل رَّبِّ ٱغْفِرْ وَٱرْحَمْ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ (۱۱۸)
Say, ‘My Lord, forgive and have mercy, and You are the best of the merciful.’ (118)

Key Points

At the beginning of this Surah, under Ayah 1, we mentioned a group of the successful (the prosperous). At the end of the Surah, by paying attention to the phrase "لا یفلح" (they will not succeed), we also remind [the reader] of a group of the losers: The oppressors. «لا یفلح الظالمون» [126] The criminals/sinners. «لا یفلح المجرمون» [127] The sorcerers. «لا یفلح الساحرون» [128] The disbelievers. «لا یفلح الكافرون» [129] Those who invent lies against Allah. «انّ الّذین یفترون على اللّه الكذب لا یفلحون» [130]

126) Al-An'am, 135. 127) Yunus, 17. 128) Yunus, 77. 129) Al-Mu'minun, 117. 130) Yunus, 69.

Messages

1- Allah does not perform futile or vain acts. «فتعالى اللّه»

2- The true sovereignty belongs to Allah. «الملك الحقّ»

3- Existence is under divine nurturing and management. «ربّ العرش» (Lord of the Throne), and elsewhere we read: «ربّ الناس» (Lord of mankind), «ربّ العالمین» (Lord of the worlds), and «ربّ كلّ شى ء» (Lord of everything).

4- Polytheism, whatever form it takes, is condemned. «یدع مع اللّه الهاً»

5- The polytheist lacks logic. «لا برهان له» (A person's beliefs and actions must be based on evidence and proof.)

6- Even the infallible Prophet needs Allah's mercy and forgiveness. «قل ربّ اغفر وارحم»

7- In supplication (Du'a), let us not forget the praise of Allah. «ربّ اغفر وارحم و انت خیرالرّاحمین»

«الحمدللّه ربّ العالمین»
